name: Spain
contextBlurb: Spain applies the EU MiCA framework. The CNMV (conduct and markets) and Banco de España (prudential for EMIs) supervise the parts relevant to stablecoins. Licensed firms can offer e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) to retail users. Many non-MiCA-compliant stablecoins were removed from EU platforms around March 2025 as exchanges reacted to MiCA's application and supervisory expectations. BBVA publicly announced crypto services in Spain.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
CNMV adopts five ESMA MiCA Guidelines
Major EU exchanges delisted non-MiCA-compliant stablecoins (for example, Binance announcements).
Spain aligned crypto advertising with MiCA (easing earlier national rules).

keyFigures.name: CNMV (Comisión Nacional del Mercado de Valores)
keyFigures.role: Conduct supervisor for CASPs, white papers, and advertising/consumer protection.

keyFigures.name: Banco de España
keyFigures.role: Prudential supervisor for EMIs and credit institutions (EMT issuers). Operated the VASP AML register pre-MiCA.

news.headline: Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Spain applies the EU MiCA framework. The CNMV (conduct and markets) and Banco de España (prudential for EMIs) supervise the parts relevant to stablecoins. Licensed firms can offer e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) to retail users. Many non-MiCA-compliant stablecoins were removed from EU platforms around March 2025 as exchanges reacted to MiCA's application and supervisory expectations. BBVA publicly announced crypto services in Spain.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: E-money for single-fiat EMTs or regulated crypto-asset (ART) under MiCA. Not legal tender.
